text_original: Motse said: The purpose of the magnanimous is to be found in procuring benefits for the world and eliminating its calamities.
text_normalized: Motse said: The purpose of the magnanimous is to be found in procuring benefits for the world and eliminating its calamities.

text_original: But what are the benefits of the world and w’hat its calamities?
text_normalized: But what are the benefits of the world and w’hat its calamities?

text_original: Motse said: Mutual attacks among states, mutual usurpa-tion among houses, mutual injuries among individuals.; the lack of grace and loyalty between ruler and ruled, the lack of affection and filial piety between father and son, the lack of harmony between elder and younger brothers — these are the major calamities in the world.
text_normalized: Motse said: Mutual attacks among states, mutual usurpa-tion among houses, mutual injuries among individuals.; the lack of grace and loyalty between ruler and ruled, the lack of affection and filial piety between father and son, the lack of harmony between elder and younger brothers — these are the major calamities in the world.

text_original: But whence did these calamities arise, out of mutual love?
text_normalized: But whence did these calamities arise, out of mutual love?

text_original: Motse said: They arise out of want of mutual love.
text_normalized: Motse said: They arise out of want of mutual love.

text_original: At present feudal lords have learned only to love their own states and not those of others.
text_normalized: At present feudal lords have learned only to love their own states and not those of others.

text_original: Therefore they do not scruple about attacking other states.
text_normalized: Therefore they do not scruple about attacking other states.

text_original: The heads of houses have learned only to love their own houses and not those of others.
text_normalized: The heads of houses have learned only to love their own houses and not those of others.

text_original: Therefore they do not scruple about usurping other houses.
text_normalized: Therefore they do not scruple about usurping other houses.

text_original: And individuals have learned only to love themselves and not others.
text_normalized: And individuals have learned only to love themselves and not others.

text_original: Therefore they do not scruple about injuring others.
text_normalized: Therefore they do not scruple about injuring others.

text_original: When feudal lords do not love one another there will be war on the fields.
text_normalized: When feudal lords do not love one another there will be war on the fields.

text_original: When heads of houses do not love one another they will usurp one another’s power.
text_normalized: When heads of houses do not love one another they will usurp one another’s power.

text_original: When individuals do not love one another they will injure one another.
text_normalized: When individuals do not love one another they will injure one another.

text_original: When ruler and ruled do not love one another they will not be gracious and loyal.
text_normalized: When ruler and ruled do not love one another they will not be gracious and loyal.

text_original: When father and son do not love each other they will not be affectionate and filial When elder and younger brothers do not love each other they will not be harmonious.
text_normalized: When father and son do not love each other they will not be affectionate and filial When elder and younger brothers do not love each other they will not be harmonious.

text_original: When nobody in the world loves any other, naturally the strong will overpower the weak, the many will oppress the few, the wealthy will mock the poor, the honoured will disdain the humble, the cunning will deceive the simple.
text_normalized: When nobody in the world loves any other, naturally the strong will overpower the weak, the many will oppress the few, the wealthy will mock the poor, the honoured will disdain the humble, the cunning will deceive the simple.

text_original: Therefore all the calamities, strifes, complaints, and hatred in the world have arisen out of want of mutual love.
text_normalized: Therefore all the calamities, strifes, complaints, and hatred in the world have arisen out of want of mutual love.

text_original: Therefore the benevolent disapproved of this want.
text_normalized: Therefore the benevolent disapproved of this want.

text_original: Wow that there is disapproval, how can have the condition altered?
text_normalized: Wow that there is disapproval, how can have the condition altered?

text_original: Motse said it is to be altered by the way of universal love and mutual aid.
text_normalized: Motse said it is to be altered by the way of universal love and mutual aid.

text_original: But what is the way of universal love and mutual aid?
text_normalized: But what is the way of universal love and mutual aid?

text_original: Motse said: It is to regard the state of others as one’s own, the houses of others as one’s own, the persons of others as one’s self.
text_normalized: Motse said: It is to regard the state of others as one’s own, the houses of others as one’s own, the persons of others as one’s self.

text_original: When feudal lords love one another there will be no more war; when heads of houses love one another there will be no more mutual usurpation; when individuals love on-afibther there will be no more mutual injury.
text_normalized: When feudal lords love one another there will be no more war; when heads of houses love one another there will be no more mutual usurpation; when individuals love on-afibther there will be no more mutual injury.

text_original: When ruler and ruled love each other they will be gracious and loyal; when father and son love each other they will be affectionate and filial; when elder and younger brothers love each other they will be harmonious.
text_normalized: When ruler and ruled love each other they will be gracious and loyal; when father and son love each other they will be affectionate and filial; when elder and younger brothers love each other they will be harmonious.

text_original: When all the people in the world love one another, then the strong will not overpower the weak, the many will not oppress the few, the wealthy will not mock the poor, the honoured will not disdain the humble, and the cunning will not deceive the simple.
text_normalized: When all the people in the world love one another, then the strong will not overpower the weak, the many will not oppress the few, the wealthy will not mock the poor, the honoured will not disdain the humble, and the cunning will not deceive the simple.

text_original: And it is all due to mutual love that calamities, strifes, complaints, and hatred are prevented from arising.
text_normalized: And it is all due to mutual love that calamities, strifes, complaints, and hatred are prevented from arising.

text_original: Therefore the benevolent exalt it.
text_normalized: Therefore the benevolent exalt it.

text_original: Bnt the gentlemen of the world would say: So far so good.
text_normalized: Bnt the gentlemen of the world would say: So far so good.

text_original: It is of course very excellent when love becomes universal.
text_normalized: It is of course very excellent when love becomes universal.

text_original: But it is only a difficult and distant ideal." Motse said: This is simply because the gentlemen of the world do not recognize what is to the benefit of the world, or understand what is its calamity.
text_normalized: But it is only a difficult and distant ideal." Motse said: This is simply because the gentlemen of the world do not recognize what is to the benefit of the world, or understand what is its calamity.

text_original: Now, to besiege a city, to fight in the fields, or to achieve a name at the cost of death — these are what men find difficult.
text_normalized: Now, to besiege a city, to fight in the fields, or to achieve a name at the cost of death — these are what men find difficult.

text_original: Yet when the superior encourages them, the multitude can do them.
text_normalized: Yet when the superior encourages them, the multitude can do them.

text_original: Besides, universal love and mutual aid is quite different from these.
text_normalized: Besides, universal love and mutual aid is quite different from these.

text_original: Whoever loves others is loved by others; who-ever benefits others is benefited by others; whoever hates others is hated by others; whoever injures others is injured by others.
text_normalized: Whoever loves others is loved by others; who-ever benefits others is benefited by others; whoever hates others is hated by others; whoever injures others is injured by others.

text_original: Then, what difficulty is there with it (universal love)?
text_normalized: Then, what difficulty is there with it (universal love)?

text_original: Only, the ruler fails to embody it in his government and the ordinary man in his conduct.
text_normalized: Only, the ruler fails to embody it in his government and the ordinary man in his conduct.

text_original: Formerly, Lord Wen of the state of Chin (about 630 b.c.) liked the uncouth uniform of the soldier.
text_normalized: Formerly, Lord Wen of the state of Chin (about 630 b.c.) liked the uncouth uniform of the soldier.

text_original: And so all his ministers and officers wore sheepskin jackets, carried their swords in leather girdles, and put on silk-spun hats.
text_normalized: And so all his ministers and officers wore sheepskin jackets, carried their swords in leather girdles, and put on silk-spun hats.

text_original: Thus attired, they attended the Lord when they went in and paced the court when they stayed out.
text_normalized: Thus attired, they attended the Lord when they went in and paced the court when they stayed out.

text_original: What was the reason for this?
text_normalized: What was the reason for this?

text_original: It was that what the ruler encourages the ruled will carry out.
text_normalized: It was that what the ruler encourages the ruled will carry out.

text_original: And Lord Ling of the state of Ch’u (about officers limited themselves to a single meal (a day).
text_normalized: And Lord Ling of the state of Ch’u (about officers limited themselves to a single meal (a day).

text_original: They tied their belts after exhaling, and could not stand np without leaning against the wall.
text_normalized: They tied their belts after exhaling, and could not stand np without leaning against the wall.

text_original: Within a year the court looked grim and dark.
text_normalized: Within a year the court looked grim and dark.

text_original: What was the reason for this I It was that what the ruler encourages the ruled will carry out.
text_normalized: What was the reason for this I It was that what the ruler encourages the ruled will carry out.

text_original: Again, Lord Kou Chien of the state of Yiieh (about 480 b.o.) liked the warrior’s courage, and trained his subjects accordingly.
text_normalized: Again, Lord Kou Chien of the state of Yiieh (about 480 b.o.) liked the warrior’s courage, and trained his subjects accordingly.

text_original: He had his palace boat set on fire.
text_normalized: He had his palace boat set on fire.

text_original: To test his soldiers he proclaimed that all the treasures of the state were contained therein.
text_normalized: To test his soldiers he proclaimed that all the treasures of the state were contained therein.

text_original: And he beat the drum himself to urge them on.
text_normalized: And he beat the drum himself to urge them on.

text_original: Hearing the dram the soldiers rushed on in disorder.
text_normalized: Hearing the dram the soldiers rushed on in disorder.

text_original: More than a hundred strong perished in the flames.
text_normalized: More than a hundred strong perished in the flames.

text_original: Thereupon the Lord beat the gong to let them retreat.
text_normalized: Thereupon the Lord beat the gong to let them retreat.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: Now, things like scantily died coarse clothing and the achievement of a name at the cost of death are those in which people find difficulty.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: Now, things like scantily died coarse clothing and the achievement of a name at the cost of death are those in which people find difficulty.

text_original: Yet when the ruler encourages them the multitude can stand them.
text_normalized: Yet when the ruler encourages them the multitude can stand them.

text_original: Besides, universal love and mutual aid are different from these.
text_normalized: Besides, universal love and mutual aid are different from these.

text_original: Who-ever loves others is loved by others; whoever benefits others is benefited by others; whoever hates others is hated by others; whoever injures others is injured by others.
text_normalized: Who-ever loves others is loved by others; whoever benefits others is benefited by others; whoever hates others is hated by others; whoever injures others is injured by others.

text_original: Then what difficulty is there with it (universal love)?
text_normalized: Then what difficulty is there with it (universal love)?

text_original: Only, the ruler fails to embody it in his government and the ordinary man in his conduct.
text_normalized: Only, the ruler fails to embody it in his government and the ordinary man in his conduct.

text_original: Nevertheless, the gentlemen in the empire think that, though it would be an excellent thing if love can be universalized, it is something quite impracticable.
text_normalized: Nevertheless, the gentlemen in the empire think that, though it would be an excellent thing if love can be universalized, it is something quite impracticable.

text_original: It is like carrying Mt.
text_normalized: It is like carrying Mt.

text_original: T’ai and leaping over the Chi River.
text_normalized: T’ai and leaping over the Chi River.

text_original: Motse said: The illustration is a faulty one.
text_normalized: Motse said: The illustration is a faulty one.

text_original: Of course to be able to carry Mt.
text_normalized: Of course to be able to carry Mt.

text_original: T’ai and leap over the CM River would be an extreme feat of strength.
text_normalized: T’ai and leap over the CM River would be an extreme feat of strength.

text_original: Such has never been performed from antiquity to the present time.
text_normalized: Such has never been performed from antiquity to the present time.

text_original: But universal love and mutual aid are quite different from this.
text_normalized: But universal love and mutual aid are quite different from this.

text_original: And the ancient sage-kings did practise it.
text_normalized: And the ancient sage-kings did practise it.

text_original: How do we know they did?
text_normalized: How do we know they did?

text_original: When Yii was working to bring the Deluge under control, he dug the West River and the Yu Ton River in the west in order to let off the water from the Ch'ii, Sun, and Huang Rivers.
text_normalized: When Yii was working to bring the Deluge under control, he dug the West River and the Yu Ton River in the west in order to let off the water from the Ch'ii, Sun, and Huang Rivers.

text_original: In the north he built a dam across the Yuan and Ku Rivers in order to fill the Hou Chih Ti (a basin) and the Hu Ch'ih River.
text_normalized: In the north he built a dam across the Yuan and Ku Rivers in order to fill the Hou Chih Ti (a basin) and the Hu Ch'ih River.

text_original: Ti Chu was made use of as a water divide, and a tunnel was dug through Mt, Lung Men.
text_normalized: Ti Chu was made use of as a water divide, and a tunnel was dug through Mt, Lung Men.

text_original: All these were done to benefit the peoples west of the (Yellow) River and various barbarian tribes, Yen, Tai, Hu, Ho, of the north.
text_normalized: All these were done to benefit the peoples west of the (Yellow) River and various barbarian tribes, Yen, Tai, Hu, Ho, of the north.

text_original: In the east he drained the great Plain and built dykes along the Meng Ghu River.
text_normalized: In the east he drained the great Plain and built dykes along the Meng Ghu River.

text_original: The water-course was divided into nine canals in order to regulate the water in the east and in order to benefit the people of the District of Chi.
text_normalized: The water-course was divided into nine canals in order to regulate the water in the east and in order to benefit the people of the District of Chi.

text_original: In the south he completed the Yangtze, Han, Huai, and Ju Rivers.
text_normalized: In the south he completed the Yangtze, Han, Huai, and Ju Rivers.

text_original: These ran eastward and emptied themselves into the Eive Lakes.
text_normalized: These ran eastward and emptied themselves into the Eive Lakes.

text_original: This was done in order to benefit the peoples of Ching, Ch’u, Kan, Yiieh, and the barbarians of the south.
text_normalized: This was done in order to benefit the peoples of Ching, Ch’u, Kan, Yiieh, and the barbarians of the south.

text_original: All these are the deeds of Yii.
text_normalized: All these are the deeds of Yii.

text_original: We can, then, universalize love in conduct.
text_normalized: We can, then, universalize love in conduct.

text_original: When King Wen was ruling the Western land, he shone forth like the sun and the moon all over the four quarters as well as in the Western land.
text_normalized: When King Wen was ruling the Western land, he shone forth like the sun and the moon all over the four quarters as well as in the Western land.

text_original: He did not allow the big state to oppress the small state, he did not allow the multitude to oppress the single-handed, he did not allow the influential and strong to take away the grain and liye stock from the farmers.
text_normalized: He did not allow the big state to oppress the small state, he did not allow the multitude to oppress the single-handed, he did not allow the influential and strong to take away the grain and liye stock from the farmers.

text_original: Heaven visited iiim with blessing.
text_normalized: Heaven visited iiim with blessing.

text_original: And, therefore, the old and childless had the wherewithal to spend their old age, the solitary and brotherless had the opportunity to join in the social life of men, and the orphans had the support for their growth.
text_normalized: And, therefore, the old and childless had the wherewithal to spend their old age, the solitary and brotherless had the opportunity to join in the social life of men, and the orphans had the support for their growth.

text_original: This was what King Wen had accomplished.
text_normalized: This was what King Wen had accomplished.

text_original: We can, then, universalize love in conduct.
text_normalized: We can, then, universalize love in conduct.

text_original: When King Wu was about to do service to Mt.
text_normalized: When King Wu was about to do service to Mt.

text_original: T’ai it was recorded thus: Blessed is Mt.
text_normalized: T’ai it was recorded thus: Blessed is Mt.

text_original: Duke of Chou by a long descent is about to perform his duty.
text_normalized: Duke of Chou by a long descent is about to perform his duty.

text_original: As I have obtained the approval of Heaven, the magnanimous arise to save the people of Shang Hsia as well as the barbarians (from the tyranny of Emperor Chow).
text_normalized: As I have obtained the approval of Heaven, the magnanimous arise to save the people of Shang Hsia as well as the barbarians (from the tyranny of Emperor Chow).

text_original: Though (Emperor Chow) has many near relatives, they cannot compare with the magnanimous.
text_normalized: Though (Emperor Chow) has many near relatives, they cannot compare with the magnanimous.

text_original: If there is sin anywhere, I am solely responsible." 2 This relates the deeds of King Wu.
text_normalized: If there is sin anywhere, I am solely responsible." 2 This relates the deeds of King Wu.

text_original: We can, then, universalize love in conduct.
text_normalized: We can, then, universalize love in conduct.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to be wealthy and dislike to have it poor, desire to have it orderly and dislike to have it chaotic, they should bring about universal love and mutual aid.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: If the rulers sincerely desire the empire to be wealthy and dislike to have it poor, desire to have it orderly and dislike to have it chaotic, they should bring about universal love and mutual aid.

text_original: This is the way of the sage-kings and the way to order for the world, and it should not be neglected.
text_normalized: This is the way of the sage-kings and the way to order for the world, and it should not be neglected.
